user_query:
I need a cross-team synchronization report to help the UX designer and the programmer triage issues in our XML-based app. Using only the files under input/, please produce two deliverables under out/:

1) out/triage.csv
- Include only ERROR and WARNING lines parsed from input/validation_output.txt (ignore INFO and other severities).
- Columns: timestamp, severity, file, line, owner, element, message.
- Parse fields from lines in the form: "[YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS] SEVERITY filename:line message ...", where element is the tag name if one appears in angle brackets (e.g., <order> -> order); leave element empty if not present.
- Map owner using input/ownership.json, whose file paths are relative to the input/ directory (e.g., "xml/users.xml"). If a file is not found in the mapping, set owner to "Unassigned".
- Sort rows by severity (ERROR before WARNING), then by owner (A–Z), then by file name (A–Z), then by line (numeric ascending).

2) out/summary.json
- For every .xml file in input/xml/, compute and include a by_file entry with:
  - file (e.g., "xml/users.xml"), owner, element_count (count of XML elements), attribute_count (count of all attributes), unique_elements (sorted array of distinct tag names), missing_string_keys (count of <string> elements missing a key attribute; if a file contains no <string> elements, this should be 0), error_count, warning_count (from the parsed validation output).
- Include aggregates:
  - totals: { files, elements, attributes, errors, warnings }.
  - by_owner: object keyed by owner with { total_files, total_elements, total_attributes, total_errors, total_warnings }.

Notes:
- Do not use the Internet. Work only with the provided files. All output paths are under out/.
- Ensure the counts in summary.json are consistent with the rows in triage.csv (e.g., error/warning counts per file and per owner can be recomputed from the CSV).
- The deliverables must be reproducible from the given inputs and fully self-contained. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
